title: In 1518, a Town Danced Itself to Death
script: This actually happened?! In July 1518, in Strasbourg, a woman named Frau Troffea stepped into the street and began to dance. She did not stop. Within a week, 34 more people joined her. Within a month, around 400 people were dancing — and they could not stop. City records show some danced for days, collapsing from strokes, heart attacks, and exhaustion. Panicked officials made it worse: they decided more dancing was the cure, built a wooden stage, and hired musicians. People kept dying. One theory says it was ergot, a toxic mold on rye bread. Another says it was mass stress-induced hysteria after famine and disease. Records confirm the dancing was real, but the cause is still debated. So what actually forced 400 people in Strasbourg to dance themselves to death?
